data.narrative:
Zephyr Fusion is a pre-seed nuclear energy company founded by two scientists with deep plasma physics and experimental backgrounds (Burke: 15+ years in diagnostics at Lawrence Livermore and UW-Madison; Hinson: 20+ years in modeling and plasma physics at Oak Ridge and UW-Madison). The target sector includes 3 public comps (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 5 private peers (Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, Deep Atomic), though none are sufficiently close in stage or valuation tier for reliable peer-median triangulation.

The analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the only applicable approach given data constraints. Starting from $500K in total raised as a valuation proxy, applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Pre-Seed to next-stage advancement yields $1.25M. This method assumes the company's trajectory will track standard progression rates for the sector.

The triangulated range is $1.06M to $1.44M, with a median of $1.25M. The narrow range reflects dependency on a single method rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent financials, burn rate, and runway; absence of technical validation milestones; and limited comparability to the broader nuclear energy peer set at this stage. A valuation in the $1M range is supportable given current data, but any refinement will require additional metrics on development progress and capital efficiency.
